back: Programming paradigm using pure functions and immutable data. Avoids side effects. Functions are first-class objects.
Function that always returns same output for same input, has no side effects. Easier to test and reason about.
Anonymous function: lambda args: expression. Often used with map(), filter(), sorted(). Limited to single expression.
Applies function to every item in iterable. map(function, iterable) returns iterator of results.
Creates iterator from elements satisfying condition. filter(function, iterable) returns items where function is True.
Accumulates values. from functools import reduce. reduce(function, iterable) returns single accumulated value.
Concise way to create lists: [expr for item in iterable if condition]. More readable than map/filter.
Like list comprehension but with parentheses: (expr for item in iterable). Lazy evaluation, memory efficient.
Combines iterables into tuples. zip([1,2], ['a','b']) returns [(1,'a'), (2,'b')]
Adds index to iterable. enumerate(['a','b']) returns [(0,'a'), (1,'b')]
any(iterable) returns True if any element True. all(iterable) returns True if all elements True.
Adds all items in iterable. sum([1,2,3]) returns 6. Can provide start value: sum(list, 10)
Returns sorted list. sorted(iterable, key=function, reverse=True). key specifies sorting criteria.
Functions are objects. Can be assigned to variables, passed as arguments, returned from functions.
Function that takes function(s) as argument or returns function. Examples: map, filter, decorator
